_note: Slug -> recursive.eco grammar UUID for the iching channel. Imported 2026-07-16 via the recursive.eco MCP (import_grammars, from this repo's raw main URLs) as PRIVATE DRAFTS — publish via set_grammar_visibility, then move the slug into _public_now. Preview links (no extra hosting needed): Play = https://flow.recursive.eco/play?id=<id> | Cards = https://recursive.eco/pages/grammar-viewer.html?type=custom&id=<id> | Study = https://recursive.eco/pages/study-viewer.html?type=custom&id=<id> | Tree = https://recursive.eco/pages/tree-viewer.html?type=custom&id=<id>. Mirrors recursive-tarot/tarot/_eco_ids.json and Recursive-astrology/ids.json.

_id_scheme_note: TWO id conventions now coexist: the book-grammars (zhouyi, ten-wings) use hexagram-<kw>-<pinyin> in simplified characters (historical-layers axis, aligned for the time rail); the builder's structural grammars (emergent-structure, leibniz-binary-tree) use hexagram-<n> / trigram-<name> / <x>-line in traditional characters (compositional axis, Lines→Trigrams→Hexagrams via composite_of). These are different axes, not a conflict — but cross-axis viewing needs a king_wen<->id map. RECONCILIATION DECISION PENDING (builder): unify ids, or keep both axes with a bridge on king_wen.
